title: Jaisalmer temperatures rise above 46°C as Rajasthan faces severe heatwave - The News Mill
full_text:
Extreme heat has settled over Rajasthan, with warnings in place for a severe heatwave in West Rajasthan and heatwave conditions in East Rajasthan until May 30. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported that Jaisalmer district recorded temperatures exceeding 46°C on Sunday, making it one of the hottest areas in the state.
In response, the municipal corporation has deployed water sprinklers on the streets to mitigate the effects of the heat. Agro-meteorologist Praveen Chaudhary explained, “Jaisalmer and nearby districts, temperatures are reaching 45-48°C. Locals call this period ‘Nau Tapa,’ (a nine day period of extreme heat). Scientists attribute the extreme rise to a Super El Nino effect, which has created dry conditions with no moisture in the air. As a result, temperatures are rising 48-50°C. While storms could bring temporary relief, high heat is expected to continue for 9-10 days, lasting till late May and early June.”
The IMD defines heatwaves as a period of unusually high temperatures compared to normal expectations for a region. Multiple states are experiencing extreme heat, with temperatures surpassing 40°C. The IMD’s daily weather report and forecast bulletin indicate that heatwave conditions are likely to persist across several states in the coming days.
Heatwave to severe heatwave conditions have been reported in parts of East Uttar Pradesh and Vidarbha; heatwave conditions in areas of Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, and Himachal Pradesh; some regions of East Madhya Pradesh; and isolated pockets of Uttarakhand, Odisha, Punjab, West Uttar Pradesh, and Coastal Andhra Pradesh, according to the IMD.
Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann announced on ‘X’ that government offices and public and private schools across all class segments will operate on compressed timings from Monday, May 25, functioning strictly from 7:30 AM to 1:30 PM.
The IMD also issued a severe heatwave warning for East Uttar Pradesh until May 28. A red alert remains in place for Chhatarpur district on Sunday, where temperatures have risen above 41°C since early morning. In recent days, temperatures in Khajuraho and Nowgong have reached up to 47.4°C, with severe heatwave conditions persisting.
The intense heat has led to deserted local markets, as residents seek relief by consuming water and sugarcane juice. Outdoor activity has become difficult, with most people venturing out only for essential purposes.
